{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Decision: Use asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency limiting in run_tasks

The run_tasks function caps concurrency via asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrent). Each task wrapper does `async with sem:` before running the actual callable, so at most `max_concurrent` wrappers hold the semaphore at once. This is simpler and more idiomatic than manual counting or a work-queue pattern.

Why: asyncio.Semaphore integrates cleanly with cancellation — on CancelledError, the `async with sem.__aexit__` releases the semaphore automatically. A manual counter would require a try/finally in every wrapper, which is error-prone. The outer try/except catches any BaseException (CancelledError, task exceptions) from the gather, then: (1) cancels all not-done runner tasks to prevent queued-but-not-started tasks from beginning execution, (2) awaits asyncio.wait(runners) to let in-flight tasks run their cleanup code, and (3) re-raises the original exception.

Why: asyncio.gather propagates cancellation to its children but does not wait for their cleanup before raising. The explicit cancel-then-wait sequence ensures that CancelledError reaches each started runner, their try/finally or async-with cleanup executes, and the semaphore is released before we re-raise. Calling .cancel() on tasks waiting to acquire the semaphore means they never enter the body — satisfying "queued tasks must not start on cancellation."","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_cancel_async_tasks_run_37e87bfa","emotions":["satis"],"entities":["DEC","USE","BAS","THE","CAN","WHY","CAL"],"field":"general","flags":["ORIGIN"],"importance_stars":2,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"run","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.26973867416381836,"source_file":"session: implementing run_tasks in run.py","topics":["decision","try","except","baseexception","around","asyncio","gather","cancellation","handling","outer","catches","any","cancellederror","task","exceptions","from","then","1","cancels","all","not","done","runner","tasks","to","prevent","queued","but","started","beginning","execution","2","awaits","wait","runners","let","in","flight","run","their","cleanup","code","3","re","raises","original","exception","propagates","its","children","does","before","raising","explicit","cancel","sequence","ensures","reaches","each","finally","or","async","executes","semaphore","is","released","we","raise","calling","on","waiting","acquire","means","they","never","enter","body","satisfying","must","start"],"wing":"cancel-async-tasks"}]}
